verify.value: All required fields are present.

risk.desc: Potential misalignment during assembly due to loose tolerances on sliding fits.
risk.mit: Implement tighter tolerance control on sliding fit interfaces.

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress on the frame due to uneven heat distribution.
risk.mit: Optimize thermal via placement and increase thermal conductivity materials.

risk.desc: Service access may be compromised if access panels are not properly aligned.
risk.mit: Include alignment pins and visual indicators for proper panel positioning.

risk.desc: Risk of electrical interference if harness passthroughs are not adequately shielded.
risk.mit: Use shielded cables and ensure proper grounding of harness passthroughs.

risk.desc: Potential structural weakness at corners without sufficient stiffening.
risk.mit: Add additional ribs or stiffeners at critical corner locations.

physics_paths: Load_Path: Structural loads from deployed solar panels and antennas transferred through corner_posts to frame.
Flow_Path: Electrical signals from onboard electronics routed through connector_mates and harness_passthroughs.
Thermal_Path: Heat dissipation from electronic components managed via thermal vias and heat sinks integrated into the frame.
Motion_Path: Deployable rail guides allow for the extension/retraction of solar panels and antennas.
Pressure_Path: Internal pressure maintained within the CubeSat structure.
Human_Service_Access: Access panels provide serviceability for maintenance and upgrades.

generated_parts.label: Payload Optics Bench
generated_parts.spec: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts

requirements: EO payload optics bench, thermal stability, optical baffles, focal plane interface, lens mounts
Removable payload module: optics bench, focal plane, thermal baffles, inspection window
Battery, sensor, actuator, cartridge, payload, cable, or joint modules remain removable without full teardown
Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels
Access is represented by geometry: hatch, pocket, channel, fastener pattern, slot, window, or removable cover
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges
Connectors are reachable without disassembling structure, have finger/tool clearance, and mention keying or labeling to prevent mis-mating
Rails, PC/104 standoffs, payload bay, and board stack clearances are visible
Avionics, battery, antenna, and payload modules have removable trays or access panels
Design is an educational structural/electrical mockup and avoids flight qualification claims
Structure, power, avionics, comms, and payload are decomposed with simple mass/volume/power budget notes per module that sum consistently
